top_suspicious.question: What kind of liability do Partners have under Article 28(1) of the General Partn...
top_suspicious.answer: Under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004, each Partner is jointly and severally liable with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while they are a Partner, unless otherwise agreed by all other Partners.

top_suspicious.question: Under the Foundations Law DIFC Law No. 3 of 2018, what is the maximum fine for f...
top_suspicious.answer: The maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within thirty (30) days is $1,500.

top_suspicious.question: Under the Operating Law - DIFC Law No. 7 of 2018, who is responsible for appoint...
top_suspicious.answer: Under the Operating Law - DIFC Law No. 7 of 2018, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar, and they must consult the President prior to doing so.

top_suspicious.question: What is the effective date for due diligence requirements for Pre-existing Accou...
top_suspicious.answer: For Pre-existing Accounts subject to due diligence requirements, the effective date is December 31, 2016. For New Accounts subject to due diligence requirements, the effective date is January 1, 2017.

top_suspicious.question: According to Article 2 of the DIFC Personal Property Law 2005, who made this Law...
top_suspicious.answer: The Personal Property Law 2005 was made by the Ruler of Dubai.

top_suspicious.question: What does Article 16(1)(c) of the Employment Law 2019 require an Employer to kee...
top_suspicious.answer: Article 16(1)(c) of the Employment Law 2019 requires an Employer to keep records of the Employee's Remuneration (gross and net, where applicable) and the applicable Pay Period.
